Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |\n| CU-002 | 18 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |\n| CU-003 | 51 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. A separate review of monitoring outputs/logs would be needed to confirm model stability operationally.", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters and saved a quick summary report for Dr. Martinez.

Quick summary from `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`:
- Compound: NXB-7042
- Indication: Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)
- Last updated: 2026-03-28

Patient dosing:
- CU-001: 12.4 kg, 0.15 mg/kg, calculated dose 1.86 mg, q3w, next dose 2026-04-14, clearance 3.2 mL/min
- CU-002: 9.1 kg, 0.12 mg/kg, calculated dose 1.09 mg, q3w, next dose 2026-04-12, clearance 2.8 mL/min
- CU-003: 15.7 kg, 0.18 mg/kg, calculated dose 2.83 mg, q2w, next dose 2026-04-10, clearance 4.1 mL/min

On PK stability:
- From the dosing file, nothing looks obviously unstable.
- The patient notes are generally reassuring:
  - CU-001: stable, 22% tumor regression
  - CU-002: responding well, weight gain improving
  - CU-003: newly enrolled after first dose
- That said, this config file does not include actual PK model diagnostics or runtime monitoring, so I can’t fully confirm model stability from this file alone.

If you want, I can format this into a shorter clinician-facing note or an email draft for Dr. Martinez.
